ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the data was collected */,
  "target_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the target seasonal event */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Type of data (point or bin) */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unit of measurement for the data */,
  "bin_start_incl" INTEGER /* Start of the bin (inclusive) */,
  "bin_end_notincl" INTEGER /* End of the bin (not inclusive) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point or average value for the bin */,
  "data_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was collected */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., weather station, satellite, model) */,
  "quality_flag" INTEGER /* Quality or reliability of the data (0 for poor, 1 for good) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the data collection point */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the data collection point */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the data collection point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_target_id FOREIGN KEY ("target_id") REFERENCES targets ("target_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  "climate_zone" TEXT /* Climate zone of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "targets" (
  "target_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each target */,
  "target_name" TEXT /* Name of the seasonal event or target */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the target */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the seasonal event */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the seasonal event */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the event (e.g., annually, bi-annually) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("target_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON, XML) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit */,
  "abbreviation" TEXT /* Abbreviation of the unit */,
  "conversion_factor" REAL /* Conversion factor for the unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, meteorologist, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser or client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
